name: circleci
description: Inspect and control CircleCI pipelines, workflows, and jobs

envVars.name: CIRCLECI_TOKEN
envVars.description: CircleCI personal API token (User Settings > Personal API Tokens)

tools.name: List Pipelines
tools.description: List recent pipelines for a project

projectSlug.description: Project slug in the form vcs-slug/org-name/repo-name, e.g. gh/acme/web (use 'circleci' as the vcs-slug for GitLab and GitHub App projects)

branch.description: Only return pipelines for this branch

pageToken.description: Pagination token from a previous response's next_page_token

historicalSummary.omitted: trigger details, pipeline parameters, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Pipeline
tools.description: Get a pipeline by its ID

tools.name: Trigger Pipeline
tools.description: Trigger a new pipeline for a project on a branch or tag

projectSlug.description: Project slug in the form vcs-slug/org-name/repo-name, e.g. gh/acme/web

branch.description: Branch to run on (mutually exclusive with tag)

tag.description: Tag to run on (mutually exclusive with branch)

parameters.description: Pipeline parameters as a key-value object

tools.name: List Pipeline Workflows
tools.description: List the workflows of a pipeline

pageToken.description: Pagination token from a previous response's next_page_token

historicalSummary.omitted: workflow tags and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Workflow
tools.description: Get a workflow by its ID

tools.name: List Workflow Jobs
tools.description: List the jobs of a workflow with their statuses

prompts.title: Latest build status
prompts.prompt: Show the most recent CircleCI pipelines for my project and the status of their workflows.

prompts.title: Investigate a failure
prompts.prompt: Find the last failed CircleCI workflow for my project and list which jobs failed.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: CircleCI API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to CircleCI
steps.description: Go to https://app.circleci.com and sign in (the free plan with a connected GitHub repo is sufficient for testing).

steps.title: Create a personal API token
steps.description: Open User Settings > Personal API Tokens (https://app.circleci.com/settings/user/tokens) and create a token.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as CIRCLECI_TOKEN=...

steps.title: Find your project slug
steps.description: Slugs look like gh/your-org/your-repo (or circleci/<org-id>/<project-id> for GitLab and GitHub App projects). Run List Pipelines with it to verify access.

notes: Project slugs contain slashes (vcs/org/repo) and are passed verbatim in the URL path
Personal tokens act with your user's permissions across all your organizations
